channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: ICT Internship Opportunity in Italy (Disability-Reserved): Gain Hands-On Experience in Data & AI at an International Organization
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The International Training Centre of the ILO is offering a unique disability-reserved internship opportunity within its Information and Communication Technology Services (ICTS) unit. Based in Turin, this internship is designed to empower candidates with disabilities by providing practical experience in data management, analytics, and digital transformation within a global organization. This initiative reflects ITCILO’s commitment […]

The post ICT Internship Opportunity in Italy (Disability-Reserved): Gain Hands-On Experience in Data & AI at an International Organization appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Training Centre of the ILO is offering a unique disability-reserved internship opportunity within its Information and Communication Technology Services (ICTS) unit. Based in Turin, this internship is designed to empower candidates with disabilities by providing practical experience in data management, analytics, and digital transformation within a global organization.

This initiative reflects ITCILO’s commitment to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunities, particularly in creating accessible career pathways for persons with disabilities.

 About the Organization

The International Training Centre of the ILO (ITCILO) is a leading institution affiliated with the International Labour Organization, dedicated to providing training and capacity-building programs worldwide.

Its Information and Communication Technology Services (ICTS) unit plays a critical role in delivering digital solutions, data systems, and learning technologies to staff, learners, and institutional partners across the globe.

 About the Internship Role

This internship focuses on data management, analysis, and digital innovation, offering candidates the opportunity to work on real-world projects that support evidence-based decision-making within an international environment.

Interns will collaborate with cross-functional teams and contribute to improving how data is collected, managed, analyzed, and communicated.

 Learning Objectives

During the internship, selected candidates will:

  • Develop a strong understanding of data ecosystems, including governance and reporting frameworks
  • Gain hands-on experience in data cleaning, validation, and integration
  • Strengthen analytical skills through data analysis and visualization tasks
  • Learn to design dashboards and reports tailored to different stakeholders
  • Explore emerging AI and GenAI tools for data analysis
  • Work in a multicultural, multidisciplinary international environment

 Key Responsibilities

Under the supervision of the Chief Information Officer (CIO), interns will:

  • Support data quality improvement and standardization initiatives
  • Assist in developing dashboards, reports, and visualizations
  • Contribute to building data governance frameworks and documentation
  • Support automation and data integration projects
  • Evaluate AI/GenAI applications for improving workflows and reporting
  • Promote a data-driven culture through knowledge-sharing initiatives

 Skills and Competencies You Will Gain

This internship is ideal for developing:

  • Data analysis and visualization skills
  • Knowledge of digital accessibility and inclusive design
  • Understanding of data governance and systems
  • Exposure to AI tools such as ChatGPT, Copilot, or Claude
  • Experience in international and cross-cultural teamwork

 Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

📚 Education

  • Currently enrolled in or recently graduated from a graduate/postgraduate program in:
    • Computer Science
    • Data Science
    • Information Systems
    • Or a related field

🌍 Languages

  • Good working knowledge of English
  • Additional languages are a plus

💻 Technical Skills

  • Knowledge of data analysis tools (Excel, Power BI, Tableau)
  • Basic knowledge of SQL and/or Python (preferred)
  • Familiarity with cloud data systems (asset)
  • Experience with AI tools (desirable)

🌟 Soft Skills

  • Analytical thinking
  • Creativity and initiative
  • Strong digital mindset

Important Inclusion Note

This internship is exclusively reserved for candidates with disabilities. Applicants without a disability are not eligible for this specific position.

However, ITCILO encourages candidates with disabilities to apply to all its internship opportunities.

Candidates can request reasonable accommodations during the application process by contacting the organization directly. All such requests are handled confidentially and do not affect the selection outcome.

 Internship Details

  • Location: Turin, Italy (remote work may be arranged)
  • Duration: Minimum 8 weeks – Maximum 6 months
  • Start Date: Flexible (to be mutually agreed)

 Application Deadline

  • Deadline: 10 May 2026

 Why You Should Apply

This internship offers a rare opportunity to:

  • Work within a United Nations-affiliated organization
  • Build practical skills in data and AI
  • Contribute to meaningful, real-world projects
  • Gain international experience in a diverse environment
  • Advance your career in ICT, data science, or digital transformation

For candidates with disabilities, this program represents a strong step toward inclusive professional development and global career exposure.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here

The post ICT Internship Opportunity in Italy (Disability-Reserved): Gain Hands-On Experience in Data & AI at an International Organization appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Opportunities in Sustainable Agriculture 2026: Study Emissions Reduction and Farm-Level Change in Australia
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
If you are passionate about sustainability, climate action, and agricultural transformation, two exciting PhD opportunities at the University of Western Australia offer a unique pathway to contribute to global net-zero goals while advancing your academic career. These fully funded and top-up-supported doctoral projects focus on one of the most urgent global challenges: reducing emissions in […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Opportunities in Sustainable Agriculture 2026: Study Emissions Reduction and Farm-Level Change in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about sustainability, climate action, and agricultural transformation, two exciting PhD opportunities at the University of Western Australia offer a unique pathway to contribute to global net-zero goals while advancing your academic career.

These fully funded and top-up-supported doctoral projects focus on one of the most urgent global challenges: reducing emissions in agriculture. Both PhDs are embedded within cutting-edge research funded by the Zero Net Emissions Agriculture Cooperative Research Centre, bringing together leading researchers, institutions, and industry stakeholders to accelerate sustainable agricultural transitions.

 Why These PhD Opportunities Matter

Agriculture is a major contributor to global greenhouse gas emissions. Achieving net-zero targets requires not only technological innovation but also deep social transformation—especially at the farm level.

Despite the availability of low-emission technologies and practices, adoption remains slow due to:

  • Communication gaps between stakeholders
  • Lack of trust in institutions and systems
  • Social and cultural barriers within farming communities
  • Weak support networks and post-adoption challenges

These PhD projects aim to tackle these issues from a social science perspective—focusing on communication, behavioral change, institutional support, and co-designed solutions.

 PhD Opportunity 1: Co-Creating Communication Approaches for Emissions Reduction

This doctoral project explores how communication strategies can be redesigned to better support farmers in adopting low-emission practices.

Supervised by leading researchers, including Dr Heather Bray, the project places strong emphasis on collaboration, participatory research, and real-world impact.

🔍 Research Focus

You will work within a “Living Laboratory” environment, engaging directly with farmers, stakeholders, and communities to:

  • Analyze existing communication frameworks used in agriculture
  • Understand how beliefs, attitudes, and trust influence adoption
  • Identify socio-cultural barriers to effective communication
  • Co-design innovative communication strategies with producers
  • Collaborate with industry bodies to streamline messaging

🎯 Research Objectives

  • Evaluate current communication networks and systems
  • Identify social and cultural dynamics shaping communication
  • Develop and test new communication approaches for emissions reduction

🌱 Impact

This research will directly contribute to improving how sustainability practices are communicated and adopted across the agricultural sector—bridging the gap between innovation and implementation.

APPLY HERE 

 PhD Opportunity 2: Supporting Farm-Level Change and Adoption

The second PhD focuses on strengthening the systems and networks that enable farmers to adopt and sustain low-emission practices.

Rather than focusing only on adoption, this project examines what happens after change begins—ensuring long-term success and scalability.

🔍 Research Focus

Through participatory research, you will:

  • Study support systems that help farmers maintain sustainable practices
  • Analyze the role of supply chains, institutions, and service providers
  • Identify gaps in the agricultural “enabling environment”
  • Design interventions that support long-term behavioral change

🎯 Research Objectives

  • Evaluate existing support networks and institutional frameworks
  • Develop strategies to improve adoption and diffusion of innovations
  • Identify investments and policy changes needed for scaling solutions

🌱 Impact

This research will help build resilient agricultural systems that not only adopt sustainable practices but sustain them over time—ensuring real progress toward net-zero agriculture.

APPLY HERE 

 Funding and Scholarship Details

Both opportunities offer strong financial support:

💵 Main Scholarship (PhD 1)

  • Stipend: AUD $38,110 per year

➕ Top-Up Scholarship (PhD 2)

  • Additional funding: AUD $10,000 per year
  • Requires securing a base scholarship (e.g., RTP)

These funding packages are designed to support candidates throughout their PhD journey, allowing them to focus on impactful research.

 Location and Study Requirements

Both PhD positions require candidates to:

  • Be based in Perth for the duration of the program
  • Conduct fieldwork across Western Australia and Victoria
  • Engage directly with agricultural communities and stakeholders

This hands-on approach ensures that research outcomes are grounded in real-world challenges and solutions.

 Important Dates

  • Opening Date: 1 April 2026
  • Application Deadline: 31 May 2026

 Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet strict academic and research requirements, including:

  • A completed Honours or Master’s degree in a relevant field
  • Strong background in independent research (thesis required)
  • Experience in participatory or qualitative research methods
  • Knowledge of agricultural systems and/or science communication
  • Fluency in English

For international applicants:

  • Minimum CGPA of 3.9 (≈85% average)

Additionally, candidates must carefully review the university’s PhD admission policies before applying.

 Application Process

To apply:

  1. Follow the official PhD application guidelines from the University of Western Australia
  2. Prepare the following documents:
    • Academic transcripts (minimum 80% thesis grade, 85% average)
    • CV highlighting relevant experience
    • Statement of motivation (max 1 page)
    • Details of previous research (thesis title and grades)
  3. Contact the project lead, Professor Marit Kragt, via email with all required materials

⚠️ Applications that do not meet the criteria or fail to include all documents will not be considered.

 Why You Should Apply

These PhD opportunities are ideal for candidates who want to:

  • Work on real-world sustainability challenges
  • Collaborate with leading researchers and industry partners
  • Gain hands-on experience through fieldwork
  • Contribute to global climate and agricultural transformation
  • Build a career in academia, policy, or international development

With global attention on climate change and food security, expertise in sustainable agriculture is more valuable than ever.

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Fully Funded PhD Opportunities in Sustainable Agriculture 2026: Study Emissions Reduction and Farm-Level Change in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ICMM Young Leaders Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Future Sustainability Leaders
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The International Council on Mining and Metals Young Leaders Scholarship 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded opportunity designed to empower exceptional young leaders who are shaping the future of sustainability, responsible mining, and climate action. In partnership with One Young World, this scholarship offers selected participants the chance to attend the globally renowned **One Young […]

The post ICMM Young Leaders Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Future Sustainability Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Council on Mining and Metals Young Leaders Scholarship 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded opportunity designed to empower exceptional young leaders who are shaping the future of sustainability, responsible mining, and climate action. In partnership with One Young World, this scholarship offers selected participants the chance to attend the globally renowned **One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town.

This initiative is more than just a scholarship—it is a global platform to amplify youth voices, accelerate impactful solutions, and connect with world-class leaders across industries.

About the ICMM Young Leaders Scholarship

The International Council on Mining and Metals (ICMM) is a global leadership organization representing major mining and metals companies committed to improving environmental and social performance. Its mission is rooted in strengthening the sector’s contribution to sustainable development.

As the world transitions toward a net-zero economy, demand for minerals and metals is expected to rise significantly. However, this transition must be managed responsibly—ensuring environmental protection, respect for human rights, and sustainable economic development. ICMM recognizes both the potential and the historical challenges of the mining industry and aims to foster responsible practices that benefit both people and the planet.

Through this scholarship, ICMM collaborates with One Young World to identify and support young leaders who are actively working on:

  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Decarbonisation and climate action
  • Responsible natural resource production
  • Social performance and community engagement
  • Sustainability and environmental governance

 About One Young World

One Young World is a global network of over 20,000 young leaders from more than 190 countries. It serves as a platform for empowering youth to drive social impact and global change. The organization has already invested millions in supporting impactful initiatives led by young changemakers worldwide.

The One Young World Summit is its flagship annual event, bringing together delegates, global leaders, and influential figures to discuss and solve pressing global challenges.

 What Does the Scholarship Offer?

Selected candidates will receive a comprehensive package that supports their participation and leadership journey:

✈️ Fully Funded Participation

  • Access to the One Young World Summit 2026 (3–6 November 2026)
  • Coverage of travel, accommodation, and related expenses

🧠 Exclusive Leadership Opportunities

  • Direct dialogue with ICMM’s CEO during the summit
  • Participation in high-level discussions and networking sessions

🌐 Global Network Access

  • Lifelong membership in the One Young World Ambassador Community
  • Connection with a global network of changemakers and leaders

🚀 Post-Summit Development

  • Access to the One Young World Action Accelerator
  • Mentorship from global experts
  • Participation in the Global Kickstart Programme for leadership development

 Important Dates

  • Opening Date: 29 April 2026
  • Application Deadline: 7 June 2026
  • Summit Dates: 3–6 November 2026

 Application Process

Interested applicants must apply through the official application portal. The selection process may include:

  • Submission of additional documents
  • References or recommendation letters
  • A pitch video presenting your impact
  • A virtual interview with representatives from ICMM and One Young World

Applicants are encouraged to clearly demonstrate their leadership experience, commitment to sustainability, and the tangible impact of their work.

 Why You Should Apply

This scholarship is ideal for young professionals and activists who want to:

  • Expand their global influence and visibility
  • Gain access to world-class mentorship and training
  • Collaborate with like-minded leaders across sectors
  • Contribute to solving urgent environmental and social challenges
  • Strengthen their leadership capacity in sustainability and governance

Participating in the One Young World Summit can be a transformative experience—opening doors to international collaborations, funding opportunities, and long-term impact.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post ICMM Young Leaders Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Future Sustainability Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for Upwell 2026: Ocean Innovation Startup Program with $20,000 Funding and Real-World Pilot Deployment
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
The global blue economy is undergoing a powerful transformation—and Upwell 2026 is at the center of it. Designed specifically for ocean innovation startups, this unique venture program is not just another accelerator. Instead, it focuses on one of the most difficult yet critical phases of startup growth: real-world deployment. Upwell exists to bridge the gap […]

The post Apply for Upwell 2026: Ocean Innovation Startup Program with $20,000 Funding and Real-World Pilot Deployment appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The global blue economy is undergoing a powerful transformation—and Upwell 2026 is at the center of it. Designed specifically for ocean innovation startups, this unique venture program is not just another accelerator. Instead, it focuses on one of the most difficult yet critical phases of startup growth: real-world deployment.

Upwell exists to bridge the gap between prototype and commercialization by connecting founders with industry partners, funding opportunities, and structured collaboration frameworks that enable real pilot execution.

If you are building an ocean-climate solution and want to move beyond theory into real-world application, this program offers a rare and highly practical pathway.

What Is Upwell?

Upwell is a milestones-based commercialization program tailored for startups working on ocean innovation technologies. It operates in a hybrid format, combining structured support with hands-on collaboration.

Unlike traditional startup accelerators or demo-day showcases, Upwell focuses on:

  • Deployment, not just pitching
  • Industry integration, not just mentorship
  • Real pilots, not just prototypes

Its core mission is to help startups transition from prototype to pilot, ensuring that solutions are tested and validated in real operational environments across Latin America and the Caribbean.

What Makes Upwell Different?

Upwell is inspired by the natural ocean process of upwelling, where nutrients rise from deep waters to fuel ecosystems. Similarly, the program brings promising innovations to the surface and into motion.

Key differentiators include:

  1. Purpose-Built for Ocean Innovation
    • Specifically designed for ocean and climate-focused startups
  2. Prototype-to-Pilot Focus
    • Emphasis on real-world deployment rather than theoretical validation
  3. Industry-Defined Challenges
    • Startups work on real problems identified by corporate partners
  4. Catalytic Funding Support
    • Financial backing to enable pilot execution
  5. Hands-On Industry Engagement
    • Direct collaboration with operators and decision-makers

Why the Focus on Latin America and the Caribbean?

The region holds immense untapped potential in the blue economy:

  • Ocean industries generate over $400 billion annually in the Caribbean alone
  • Approximately 30% of the population lives in coastal areas
  • The region offers 5–10x cost advantages in early-stage R&D
  • It contains 26% of global mangroves and 25% of coral reefs

Despite these strengths, structural barriers exist:

  • Limited access to early-stage climate capital
  • Lack of industry partnerships for B2B startups
  • Most accelerators are based outside the region
  • High costs and risks associated with pilot deployment

Upwell directly addresses these challenges by aligning startups with real demand, funding, and deployment opportunities.

What You Get as a Startup

Selected participants gain access to a powerful ecosystem designed for real impact:

1. Industry-Aligned Use Cases

  • Clearly defined problems based on real corporate needs

2. Access to Decision-Makers

  • Direct connections with corporate partners and stakeholders

3. Pilot Design & Funding

  • Up to US$20,000 in catalytic funding for selected startups

4. Technical & Commercial Support

  • Assistance in refining both product and business models

5. Growth Pathways

  • Opportunities for follow-on investment and global market expansion

Benefits for Industry Partners

Upwell also creates value for corporate participants by:

  • Providing access to vetted, high-quality startups
  • Enabling structured collaboration from concept to pilot
  • Aligning innovation with sustainability and operational goals
  • Reducing risks in testing emerging technologies
  • Enhancing visibility as leaders in the blue economy

Program Timeline – Upwell 2026

The program follows a structured timeline to ensure meaningful progress:

Application Period

  • Opens: March 16
  • Closes: May 15 (Application Deadline)

 Review & Acceptance

  • March 16 to May 29

Diagnostics & Matching

  • May 20 to June 12
  • Startup diagnostics, corporate alignment, mentor matching

Pilot Development

  • June 15 to August 31
  • Prototype validation and pilot co-design

Showcase & Funding Kickoff

  • September
  • Industry showcase, pilot agreements, and funding activation

Eligibility and Requirements

Upwell is designed for startups that have moved beyond the idea stage and are ready for real-world testing.

Technical Validation Criteria

Your solution should demonstrate early feasibility through:

  • Bench-scale or laboratory testing
  • Functional hardware, software, or biological systems
  • Measurable performance against technical benchmarks

Additional Eligibility Notes

  • No revenue required
  • No prior pilot partner required
  • Open to individuals and pre-incorporated teams
  • Prior accelerator participation or funding is allowed

Costs and Funding

  • No participation fee for selected startups
  • Travel costs for program activities are covered
  • Catalytic funding is provided to support pilot deployment

Why You Should Apply

Upwell is not just a learning experience—it is a deployment engine. If your goal is to:

  • Validate your solution in real-world conditions
  • Secure your first industry pilot
  • Prepare for commercial scaling

Then this program offers a structured, well-supported pathway to achieve that.

How to Apply

Startups ready to take their ocean innovation to the next level can apply directly HERE.

To get more information about this opportunity, click HERE.

For inquiries: upwell@reciprocal.studio

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY  HERE.

The post Apply for Upwell 2026: Ocean Innovation Startup Program with $20,000 Funding and Real-World Pilot Deployment appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026 Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award: Win Up to $10,000 to Drive Change Through Sports
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
Sport has long been more than competition. Across schools, campuses, and communities, young leaders are transforming sports into a powerful tool for social change, inclusion, leadership, and community empowerment. The 2026 Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award is now accepting applications from exceptional young changemakers across the United States who are using sports to create […]

The post 2026 Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award: Win Up to $10,000 to Drive Change Through Sports appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Sport has long been more than competition. Across schools, campuses, and communities, young leaders are transforming sports into a powerful tool for social change, inclusion, leadership, and community empowerment. The 2026 Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award is now accepting applications from exceptional young changemakers across the United States who are using sports to create meaningful impact in their communities.

Established in 2019 as part of the prestigious Sports Humanitarian Awards and the ESPYS, this nationally recognized opportunity honors the legacy of legendary sports icon Billie Jean King by celebrating young leaders who embody courage, equity, leadership, and service through sport.

The award recognizes young people who are not only participating in sports programs but actively leading initiatives, addressing community challenges, and building opportunities for others through sport-based action and advocacy.

Applications are due by midnight EST on May 22, 2025.

What is the Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award?

The Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award is a national recognition program that celebrates youth leaders using sports as a platform to create positive social change. The initiative highlights young individuals who demonstrate leadership, inclusion, courage, and measurable community impact through sports-driven initiatives.

As part of the award program:

  • Three National Honorees will be selected
  • Twenty Regional Honorees will also be recognized
  • Honorees will participate in leadership development experiences
  • Scholarship and nonprofit grant funding will be awarded

The award is connected to the Sports Humanitarian Awards and the ESPYS in New York City, placing recipients among some of the most influential leaders and changemakers in sports.

Benefits of the 2026 Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award

The program offers financial support, national recognition, mentorship opportunities, and leadership development experiences.

Benefits for National Honorees

Three national honorees will receive:

1. National Recognition

Recipients will be honored during:

  • The Sports Humanitarian Awards
  • The ESPYS in New York City

Honorees will be recognized alongside prominent sports leaders and humanitarian award winners, including recipients of the Muhammad Ali Sports Humanitarian Award.

2. Financial Support

National honorees will receive either:

  • A one-time $10,000 college scholarship, OR
  • A $10,000 charitable grant directed to an eligible nonprofit organization connected to their work

3. Leadership Development Program

Recipients will participate in a yearlong virtual experiential learning program that includes:

  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Leadership coaching
  • Networking with senior sports industry professionals
  • Career and leadership development sessions

Benefits for Regional Honorees

Twenty regional honorees will receive:

1. Scholarship or Grant Funding

Recipients will receive either:

  • A one-time $2,500 college scholarship, OR
  • A $2,500 charitable grant directed to an eligible nonprofit organization

2. Leadership and Mentorship Opportunities

Regional honorees will also participate in the yearlong leadership and mentorship program designed to help them grow their impact and leadership capacity.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is designed for young leaders who are intentionally using sports to improve lives and communities.

Successful applicants are expected to demonstrate:

Community Leadership

Applicants should identify a challenge facing their school or community and explain how they are actively addressing it.

Examples may include:

  • Youth empowerment through sports
  • Mental health awareness initiatives
  • Gender equality in sports
  • Inclusion programs
  • Community outreach activities
  • Anti-bullying campaigns
  • Leadership development through athletics

Meaningful Use of Sport for Change

Applicants must show how sports are being used as a deliberate tool for social impact rather than simply participating in existing programs.

Strong applicants are those who:

  • Founded initiatives
  • Led community projects
  • Developed sports-based outreach programs
  • Expanded opportunities for underserved communities

Measurable Impact

Candidates should clearly explain:

  • Their goals and objectives
  • Community outcomes achieved
  • Evidence of impact
  • Positive changes resulting from their work

Strong Personal Values

The award seeks individuals who reflect Billie Jean King’s values, including:

  • Courage
  • Equity
  • Inclusion
  • Leadership
  • Empowerment
  • Service

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the 2026 Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award, applicants must meet the following requirements:

General Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be a U.S. citizen, U.S. national, or permanent resident
  • Be between the ages of 16 and 25
  • Be enrolled as a high school, college, or graduate student

Students taking a gap year are also eligible if they:

  • Have graduated from high school
  • Will enroll in an accredited four-year college or university during the 2026–2027 academic year

Applicants must also:

  • Have a registered and verifiable 501(c)(3) nonprofit affiliated with their work
  • Ensure the nonprofit and community impact work are U.S.-based

Additional Scholarship Eligibility Requirements

Applicants choosing the scholarship option must additionally:

  • Demonstrate financial need verified by their college or university
  • Have a minimum cumulative GPA of 2.5 on a 4.0 scale
  • Be enrolled in an accredited four-year college or university during the 2026–2027 academic year

Important Application Rules

Applicants should carefully note the following guidelines:

Single Applications Only

  • Only individual nominations will be accepted
  • Group applications are not allowed

Application Submission

Applications may be submitted:

  • Directly by the nominee, OR
  • By a nonprofit organization on behalf of the nominee

Photo Submission Requirements

Selected applicants will later be required to submit high-resolution images, including:

  • A professional headshot
  • A front-facing action photo
  • A full-body image without other people

Group photos will not be accepted.

Funding Guidelines

Scholarship Funding

Scholarships are:

  • One-time awards
  • Non-renewable
  • Paid directly to the recipient’s accredited institution

Nonprofit Grant Funding

Grant funds will be directed to:

  • A verified 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization
  • Organizations that meet The Walt Disney Company’s charitable giving guidelines

Applicants can review the charitable giving guidelines here:
https://impact.disney.com/app/uploads/2025/04/Global-Charitable-Giving-Guidelines.pdf

Information Sessions and Webinars

To support applicants during the process, several information webinars will be hosted.

Webinar Registration Dates

Saturday, May 2 – 11 AM EST

https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/register/WX0jxh3XSLyqGKxXY4MFhw

Thursday, May 7 – 1 PM EST

https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/register/So6cc4btT_W822BpN7QeeQ

Saturday, May 16 – 11 AM EST

https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/register/ZgVmoq5lQqSLmnZuvzAk0w

Monday, May 18 – 1 PM EST

https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/register/s41LegEPQdeexxAZhaXkgA

Need Help?

Applicants with questions about the award can contact:

  • Email: claudia.larrea@beyondsport.org
  • Meeting Link: https://tinyurl.com/ubxjmsbx

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award is more than financial support or recognition. It is a platform that amplifies the voices of young leaders who are shaping the future of sports and social impact.

For young changemakers passionate about equity, leadership, and community transformation, this award provides:

  • National visibility
  • Leadership development
  • Mentorship
  • Funding support
  • Access to influential networks in sports and social impact

Young leaders using sports to create change are encouraged to apply and share their stories before the deadline.

Apply Here

Application Deadline

Applications close at midnight EST on May 22, 2025.

For more information about this click here

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post 2026 Billie Jean King Youth Leadership Award: Win Up to $10,000 to Drive Change Through Sports appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Tsinghua International Summer School on Environment 2026: Fully Funded Global Program in Beijing Focused on Carbon Neutrality and Sustainability
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
The Tsinghua University International Summer School on Environment 2026 is a prestigious and fully funded global academic program designed to bring together students from around the world to explore critical environmental challenges. Hosted by the School of Environment (SOE), this summer school serves as a powerful platform for cross-cultural exchange, academic enrichment, and global collaboration […]

The post Tsinghua International Summer School on Environment 2026: Fully Funded Global Program in Beijing Focused on Carbon Neutrality and Sustainability appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Tsinghua University International Summer School on Environment 2026 is a prestigious and fully funded global academic program designed to bring together students from around the world to explore critical environmental challenges. Hosted by the School of Environment (SOE), this summer school serves as a powerful platform for cross-cultural exchange, academic enrichment, and global collaboration in the field of environmental sustainability.

As environmental concerns like climate change, carbon emissions, and ecological preservation continue to shape global conversations, this program offers participants a unique opportunity to engage directly with these issues while experiencing life and learning in China.

Why Choose Tsinghua’s International Summer School on Environment?

Summer schools at Tsinghua University are an integral part of its mission to foster international collaboration and academic excellence. This program is specifically tailored to:

  • Promote cross-cultural communication and global connections
  • Provide a platform for students worldwide to engage with China academically and culturally
  • Encourage participants to expand their global perspectives
  • Support the development of future leaders in environmental sustainability

Program Essentials

  • Program Name: Tsinghua International Summer School on Environment 2026
  • Host Institution: Tsinghua University
  • Location: Beijing, China
  • Duration: July 3 – July 17, 2026

Important Dates

  • First Round Application Deadline: April 15, 2026
  • Second Round Application Deadline: May 10, 2026
  • Results Notification: May 20, 2026

Financial Support

This is a fully funded opportunity, supported by the China Scholarship Council. Selected participants will receive:

  • Full waiver of registration fees
  • Coverage of accommodation costs in Beijing
  • Sponsorship for living expenses during the program

This ensures that students can focus entirely on learning, networking, and cultural exchange without financial burden.

Program Theme and Academic Focus

The 2026 program centers on:

  • Carbon Neutrality
  • Environmental Protection

Participants will explore these themes through a combination of academic lectures, collaborative projects, and real-world applications.

Program Highlights

1. Global Recognition

  • Participants will receive an official certificate from Tsinghua University
  • Enhance academic and professional profiles with an internationally respected credential

2. Innovation and Real-World Impact

  • Work on solving real environmental challenges
  • Collaborate with international peers
  • Participate in SDG (Sustainable Development Goals) innovation projects

3. Interdisciplinary Learning

  • Courses led by top faculty from Tsinghua’s School of Environment
  • Exposure to diverse perspectives across environmental disciplines

4. Field Trips and Technical Visits

  • Visit cutting-edge environmental facilities
  • Gain firsthand insight into China’s sustainability practices

5. Cultural Exchange

  • Participate in traditional Chinese art workshops
  • Engage in cultural salons
  • Build lasting friendships with students from around the world

Draft Program Schedule Overview

The program is structured to balance academic learning, practical exposure, and cultural experiences:

  1. July 3: Arrival and Registration
  2. July 4: Opening Ceremony, Group Photo, Keynote Lectures
  3. July 5: Field Trip and Cultural Salon
  4. July 6: Lectures + SDG Innovation Project
  5. July 7: Lectures + Technical Visit
  6. July 8 – July 11: Lectures + SDG Innovation Projects
  7. July 12: Field Trip and Cultural Salon
  8. July 13: Lectures + SDG Innovation Project
  9. July 14: Lectures + Technical Visit
  10. July 15: SDG Innovation Project Work
  11. July 16: Final Presentation and Award Ceremony
  12. July 17: Departure

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must be:

  • Current undergraduate, graduate, or master’s students
  • From an environmental science or related academic background

Application Requirements

To apply, candidates must prepare the following documents:

  • Academic Transcript
  • Passport Copy
  • Personal Statement
  • Academic CV
  • Proof of English Proficiency
  • Letter of Recommendation (optional but recommended)

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their applications HERE.

For more information about this opportunity, click HERE.

Contact Information

  • Phone: +86–10–62796342
  • Email: hjxgs@tsinghua.edu.cn

Discover more summer school opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Tsinghua International Summer School on Environment 2026: Fully Funded Global Program in Beijing Focused on Carbon Neutrality and Sustainability appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Charles Wallace Visiting Fellowship Programme 2026: Fully Funded UK Fellowship for Mid-Career Pakistani Professionals
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Charles Wallace Visiting Fellowship Programme 2026, administered by the British Council in Pakistan, is a prestigious international opportunity designed to support mid-career professionals in advancing their expertise through short-term academic and professional engagement in the United Kingdom. This fellowship provides a unique platform for professionals to undertake high-level research, strengthen global networks, and gain […]

The post Charles Wallace Visiting Fellowship Programme 2026: Fully Funded UK Fellowship for Mid-Career Pakistani Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Charles Wallace Visiting Fellowship Programme 2026, administered by the British Council in Pakistan, is a prestigious international opportunity designed to support mid-career professionals in advancing their expertise through short-term academic and professional engagement in the United Kingdom.

This fellowship provides a unique platform for professionals to undertake high-level research, strengthen global networks, and gain exposure to world-class institutions across multiple disciplines. It is particularly tailored for individuals seeking to enhance their knowledge, skills, and international collaborations in fields such as social sciences, arts, humanities, environmental sciences, and medical sciences.

Overview of the Charles Wallace Visiting Fellowship Programme

The Charles Wallace Visiting Fellowship Programme aims to promote professional development and international collaboration through:

  • Advanced study and research in a UK institution
  • Professional networking with global experts and peers
  • Exposure to cutting-edge practices and knowledge systems

Fellows are hosted by universities, professional bodies, or arts organisations in the United Kingdom, allowing them to engage deeply in their field of expertise.

Fellowship Duration

The programme is designed as a short-term professional visit:

  • Minimum duration: 2 weeks
  • Maximum duration: 2 months

This flexible structure enables participants to tailor their fellowship experience according to their research or professional goals.

Fellowship Benefits and Financial Support

Selected fellows will receive financial support to facilitate their stay in the UK:

  1. Monthly Grant
    • £1,600 per month
  2. Travel and Visa Support
    • Contribution of up to £1,250 towards travel and visa costs
  3. Professional Development
    • Access to UK-based institutions and networks
    • Opportunities for collaboration, research, and skill enhancement

Note: Fellows are responsible for arranging their own accommodation and logistics in consultation with their host institution.

Eligible Fields of Study

The fellowship supports a wide range of disciplines, including:

  • Social Sciences and Humanities
  • Arts and Creative Industries
  • Environmental Sciences
  • Medical Sciences

Applicants must ensure their proposed work aligns strictly with these eligible areas.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the fellowship, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  1. Nationality and Residency
    • Must be a Pakistani national residing in Pakistan
  2. Age Requirement
    • Must be between 30 and 55 years old
  3. Academic and Professional Background
    • Should be a mid-career professional
    • Must typically hold a doctoral degree if applying for research at a university
  4. Invitation Requirement
    • Must have a formal written invitation from a UK institution or organisation
  5. Previous Study Restrictions
    • Applicants who have studied in the UK, USA, Canada, Australia, Europe, or China within the last 5 years are not eligible
    • Previous recipients of Charles Wallace awards are not eligible
  6. Application Integrity
    • Use of AI tools for drafting or editing application statements is strictly prohibited and may result in disqualification

Application Process

Applicants must follow a structured process to apply:

  1. Complete the Online Application Form
    • Access the official application portal
  2. Prepare Required Documents
    • Completed application form
    • Letter of invitation from a UK institution
    • Updated CV (including a photograph)
  3. Submit via Email
    • Send all documents to: cwpt@britishcouncil.org.pk
  4. Application Deadline
    • Friday, 5 June 2026
  5. Shortlisting and Interviews
    • Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews (in-person or online) in late June or early July 2026
  6. Programme Start
    • Successful candidates can begin their fellowship from September 2026 onwards

Important Notes:

  • Incomplete or hard-copy applications will not be accepted
  • Applicants must independently secure their UK host institution

Click HERE to apply.

Why Apply for the Charles Wallace Visiting Fellowship?

This fellowship offers significant advantages for mid-career professionals:

  • Opportunity to conduct high-level research in the UK
  • Access to global academic and professional networks
  • Enhanced career prospects through international exposure
  • Development of advanced skills and knowledge
  • Collaboration with leading experts and institutions

Key Considerations for Applicants

Applicants should note the following:

  • Securing a strong UK host institution is critical
  • Applications must be original and free from AI-generated content
  • Early preparation is essential due to the competitive nature of the programme

Contact Information

For application submission and inquiries:

  • Email: cwpt@britishcouncil.org.pk

For more information about this opportunity, click HERE

Conclusion

The Charles Wallace Visiting Fellowship Programme 2026 is a highly competitive and impactful opportunity for Pakistani professionals seeking to expand their global reach and academic or professional expertise.

By providing financial support, international exposure, and access to leading institutions, the programme empowers fellows to contribute meaningfully to their fields and bring back valuable knowledge to their home country.

For more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY, visit HERE.

The post Charles Wallace Visiting Fellowship Programme 2026: Fully Funded UK Fellowship for Mid-Career Pakistani Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Loughborough Development Trust Africa Scholarship to Study in the UK(Open to international students)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Loughborough Development Trust Africa Scholarship will cover 100% of successful applicants’ postgraduate taught course fees for one year. Location: Both campuses Eligibility: International students Discount: 100% off tuition fees for one year Degree type: Master’s degrees The Loughborough Development Trust Africa Scholarship will cover 100% of the course fees for your chosen postgraduate taught […]

The post Loughborough Development Trust Africa Scholarship to Study in the UK(Open to international students) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Loughborough Development Trust Africa Scholarship will cover 100% of successful applicants’ postgraduate taught course fees for one year.

Location: Both campuses
Eligibility: International students
Discount: 100% off tuition fees for one year
Degree type: Master’s degrees

The Loughborough Development Trust Africa Scholarship will cover 100% of the course fees for your chosen postgraduate taught course for one year (replacing any departmental or Loughborough University scholarships or bursaries you may already have been awarded).

The University will award a limited number of scholarships, and the standards required are very high.

Students will be expected to fund their travel and maintenance costs through other sources. Evidence of maintenance funds must be included in the scholarship application.

The scholarships are being funded through a combination of generous external funding and University funds.

Application process and eligibility criteria

The scholarship application procedure is an open competition. Students may only apply for the scholarship after an offer for a place has been made. Students who are in possession of an offer (conditional or unconditional) of a place on a postgraduate taught course can apply for the scholarship using the application form which is available to download below.

Applications will be initially shortlisted and the final decision on the awards will be made by a selection panel of senior staff of the University. The selection panel will use the following eligibility criteria when assessing applications:

  • Currently domiciled (permanently living) in Africa
  • Evidence of exceptional academic achievement (normally a first-class honours degree)
  • Commitment to return to their home country on completion of their postgraduate programme
  • Evidence of the ability and commitment to making a significant contribution to their home country on their return
  • Full understanding of the costs involved in coming to study and live in the UK
  • Evidence of strong motivation and initiative to secure funds to cover the remainder of the costs involved

Applicants are advised to spend considerable effort ensuring that this application reaches the selectors in a form that is clear, well presented and which reflects their abilities and motivation.

Deadline

Applications for 2025/26 entry are now open, with a deadline of 30 April 2025.

Application Process

  • Step 1: Apply for a postgraduate taught course at Loughborough University. Apply now

  • Step 2: Once you receive your conditional or unconditional offer, complete the scholarship application.

  • Step 3: Submit the Global Impact Scholarship application form to be considered for both the Loughborough Development Trust Africa Scholarship and the Global Impact Scholarship.

Scholarship applications will not be considered unless the applicant is holding an offer or a place at the time of submission (this offer may be conditional or unconditional).

Please do not submit your scholarship application until you have received your offer letter. Applications will only be considered if all parts of the form are completed in full.

Applications received after the closing date will not be considered.  A confirmation email will be sent when your application has been received. All those applying will be notified of the outcome of their application by email. If your application is shortlisted, you will be given further guidelines on what will happen next.

Any award made to an applicant holding a conditional offer will be subject to those conditions being met in full. Applicants will also be expected to provide evidence of funds to cover their travel, living and maintenance costs and may be asked to pay a deposit towards their living costs as part of the conditions of the scholarship offer.

Further information

For further details about these awards, please contact the Global Engagement Team.

How to apply

To be considered for the African Development Trust Scholarship, please complete the Global Impact Scholarship application form via the link below. By submitting this single application, you will automatically be considered for both the Loughborough Development Trust Africa Scholarship and the Global Impact Scholarship, both of which offer a 100% tuition fee waiver.

Scholarship application form

The post Loughborough Development Trust Africa Scholarship to Study in the UK(Open to international students) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: DAAD Brussels Hiring Project Leader for EU–Africa Higher Education Initiative in Brussels
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The DAAD Brussels Office is now hiring a Project Leader for the EU-funded Study and Research in Africa: Opportunities (SRiA-O) initiative. This full-time role offers experienced international development and project management professionals the opportunity to lead a strategically significant EU–Africa cooperation initiative focused on higher education and research collaboration. Based in Brussels, the successful candidate […]

The post DAAD Brussels Hiring Project Leader for EU–Africa Higher Education Initiative in Brussels appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The DAAD Brussels Office is now hiring a Project Leader for the EU-funded Study and Research in Africa: Opportunities (SRiA-O) initiative.

This full-time role offers experienced international development and project management professionals the opportunity to lead a strategically significant EU–Africa cooperation initiative focused on higher education and research collaboration.

Based in Brussels, the successful candidate will oversee implementation of a complex, multi-country project in partnership with the European Commission, African and European institutions, and international stakeholders.

About the Role

The Project Leader will be responsible for strategic and operational leadership of the SRiA-O project, ensuring effective implementation, stakeholder coordination, and accountability across multiple countries and institutions.

This position is ideal for senior professionals with extensive experience managing transnational development or education projects, particularly within EU-funded frameworks.

Key Responsibilities

The selected candidate will:

  • Lead strategic and operational management of the SRiA-O initiative
  • Coordinate partners, subcontractors, and stakeholders across Europe and Africa
  • Oversee governance processes including risk, quality, and change management
  • Manage reporting, financial oversight, and accountability systems
  • Support stakeholder engagement, communication, and external representation

Candidate Profile

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • At least 10 years of experience managing transnational cooperation projects
  • Strong experience with DG INTPA / EU-funded programmes
  • Proven collaboration with African and European partner organizations
  • Excellent coordination, drafting, and stakeholder engagement skills
  • Fluency in English and French
  • German language skills are considered an advantage

Compensation and Benefits

DAAD offers:

  • Full-time employment (38 hours/week)
  • Location: Brussels, Belgium
  • Salary: €5,500–€6,000 gross per month (depending on experience)
  • Attractive benefits package
  • International and collaborative working environment

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by:

26 May 2026

How to Apply

Submit your application to:
buero.bruessel@daad.de

Subject Line: SRiA-O Project Leader

More details and full vacancy notice,Click Here

For more job opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post DAAD Brussels Hiring Project Leader for EU–Africa Higher Education Initiative in Brussels appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Schuman Traineeship 2026–2027 at the European Parliament (EPRS): Paid EU Opportunity | Apply by 31 May
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Schuman Traineeship Programme at the European Parliament is now open for applications. This highly competitive, paid traineeship offers graduates and selected vocational candidates the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in EU policymaking, research, and institutional work. If you are interested in international policy, governance, or parliamentary processes, this is one of the most prestigious […]

The post Schuman Traineeship 2026–2027 at the European Parliament (EPRS): Paid EU Opportunity | Apply by 31 May appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Schuman Traineeship Programme at the European Parliament is now open for applications. This highly competitive, paid traineeship offers graduates and selected vocational candidates the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in EU policymaking, research, and institutional work.

If you are interested in international policy, governance, or parliamentary processes, this is one of the most prestigious entry points into EU careers.

Key Details at a Glance

  • Programme: Schuman Traineeship (EPRS – European Parliamentary Research Service)
  • Institution: European Parliamentary Research Service (EPRS)
  • Duration: October 2026 – February 2027
  • Application Deadline: 31 May (23:59 Brussels time)
  • Location: European Parliament (various EU locations)
  • Funding: Paid traineeship
  • Application Limit: Maximum of 3 positions per candidate

About the Programme

The European Parliamentary Research Service (EPRS) provides independent research, analysis, and policy support to Members of the European Parliament and its committees.

As a Schuman trainee, you will:

  • Gain first-hand experience of EU institutional processes
  • Work on real policy and research assignments
  • Develop expertise in your chosen field
  • Contribute to evidence-based policymaking

Traineeship Areas

Applicants can choose from a wide range of thematic and operational areas.

Foreign and External Policies

  • Latin America
  • Europe
  • Euromed and Middle East
  • Transatlantic Relations and Global Governance
  • Africa, Caribbean and Pacific
  • Asia, Australia and New Zealand

Parliamentary Committees

  • Foreign Affairs (AFET)
  • Human Rights (DROI)
  • Security and Defence (SEDE)
  • International Trade (INTA)
  • Development (DEVE)
  • External Policy Analysis & Support

Programme Focus Areas

  • Election Observation
  • Parliamentary Support and Capacity Building
  • Human Rights Action
  • Mediation and Dialogue Support

Horizontal Services

  • Communications and Outreach
  • Strategy and Innovation
  • Information Technology (IT)
  • Human Resources

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the Schuman traineeship, candidates must meet the following requirements:

  • Be at least 18 years old
  • Be a national of an EU Member State
    • A limited number of positions are open to non-EU nationals
  • Meet language requirements
  • Hold:
    • A university degree (EQF Level 6), or
    • A vocational qualification (EQF Levels 3–5) obtained within the EU (limited positions)
  • Provide a valid criminal record certificate
  • Not have:
    • Worked or completed a traineeship in an EU institution for more than 2 months
    • Conducted a study visit at the European Parliament Secretariat within the last 6 months

New Update: Expanded Access

The programme now includes expanded eligibility:

  • A limited number of positions are open to candidates with vocational education qualifications (EQF Levels 3, 4, or 5) obtained within the EU
  • This change broadens access beyond traditional university graduates

Application Timeline

October–February Traineeship

  • Application Period: May
  • Deadline: 31 May (23:59 Brussels time)
  • Shortlisting: 1 July – 15 July
  • Admissions: 1 July – 31 August
  • Start Date: 1 October

March–July Traineeship

  • Application Period: October
  • Deadline: 31 October
  • Start Date: 1 March

How to Apply

The application process involves two main steps:

1. Register

  • Visit the official traineeship portal
  • Select a traineeship offer
  • Create an account
  • Complete your personal details

2. Submit Application

  • Log in to your account
  • Upload:
    • CV (Europass format only)
    • Motivation letter (maximum one page per application)

Important Rules

  • You may apply for up to 3 positions only
  • Applying to more than 3 roles will result in disqualification
  • Applications cannot be edited after submission
  • No additional documents will be accepted via email

Selection Process

Shortlisting

  • Conducted by relevant Directorates-General (DGs)

Eligibility Verification

Shortlisted candidates must upload:

  • Passport or ID
  • Academic or vocational certificates
  • Criminal record

Note: You have 14 days to upload documents after being shortlisted.

Final Admission

  • Offer is sent
  • Candidate accepts
  • Official admission letter issued

Why Apply for the Schuman Traineeship?

  • Gain direct exposure to EU policymaking
  • Build a career pathway in international governance
  • Work with leading experts in research, policy, and diplomacy
  • Strengthen your profile for EU and global careers

Application Link

  • Apply here: https://ep-stages.gestmax.eu/website/application-informations

Pro Tip for Applicants

  • Use the Europass CV format correctly and clearly
  • Tailor your motivation letter to each role
  • Highlight:
    • Research and analytical skills
    • Policy or institutional experience
    • Interest in EU affairs

Final Thoughts

The Schuman Traineeship at the European Parliament is a high-impact opportunity for individuals seeking careers in policy, governance, and international affairs. With expanded eligibility and diverse thematic areas, the programme is more accessible than ever.

If you meet the criteria, ensure you apply before the 31 May deadline to secure your chance to work at the heart of the European Union.

Application Link

  • Apply here: https://ep-stages.gestmax.eu/website/application-informations

The post Schuman Traineeship 2026–2027 at the European Parliament (EPRS): Paid EU Opportunity | Apply by 31 May appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
